intro: You can request repeat prescriptions for chronic medication 24 hours a day (including weekends) in 2 ways:
patientenomgeving: You can request repeat prescriptions online via the patient portal on the website. You will need to register first. Please note that it may take up to 7 days after creating an account before it becomes active.
receptenlijn: Call your own GP's practice telephone number and select the prescription line option from the menu.
receptenlijnVragen: Name and date of birth of the person for whom the prescription is intended
Street and house number of your residential address
Name or names of the medications you are ordering (keep the boxes at hand!)
welkeMedicijnen:
You can only request prescriptions for medications that you use on the advice of your GP or specialist chronically (usually daily).

If you wish to request medications for newly occurring complaints or temporary medications - which you received for a specific period - you cannot order these medicines via the prescription line or website. For this, you can call the practice assistant during office hours; if necessary, your GP will be consulted about your request.
wanneerKlaar: If you call the prescription line or order a prescription via the website before 12:00 noon, you can 2 working days later collect your medications from your pharmacy (they will be ready from 15:00).
wanneerKlaarVoorbeelden: If you call/email on Tuesday evening, your medications will be ready at the pharmacy on Friday.
If you call/email on Friday afternoon after 12:00 or during the weekend, you can collect your medications on Wednesday after 15:00.
